description: Deep crawl websites with recursive link-following, depth control, domain filtering, and LLM-ready markdown output. Use for multi-page extraction, documentation crawling, or domain research beyond single-page fetch.

verify.command: C:/Python314/python -c "import asyncio; from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler, CrawlerRunConfig, CacheMode; async def m(): config=CrawlerRunConfig(cache_mode=CacheMode.BYPASS); async with AsyncWebCrawler() as c: r=await c.arun(url='https://example.com',config=config); print(r.success and 'Example Domain' in (r.markdown or ''))" && asyncio.run(m())

whenToUse: Invoke when Hermes needs to crawl multiple pages from a domain — following links recursively, bounded by depth and page count — and return clean markdown for research, source operationalization, or knowledge ingestion. Prefer single-page web_extract for one URL. Prefer browser tools for JS-heavy SPAs.
failureModes: Crawl4AI is not installed in the configured Python environment.
lxml build from source failed on Python 3.14 — some extraction features may be degraded.
The target URL is unreachable, returns 4xx/5xx, or blocks the crawler.
A deep crawl exceeds the bounded page limit or timeout.
No pages were successfully crawled (all failed or blocked).
The returned markdown is empty or does not match expected content.
